DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT SINCLAIR'S MOTION TO REOPEN DISCOVERY AND PLAINTIFF QUEDADO'S OPPOSITION THERETO WERE DULY CONSIDERED. IT IS NOTED THAT THE PARTIES HAVE STIPULATED TO DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF ALL RELEVANT MEDICAL RECORDS AND BILLS GENERATED IN 2018. BEFORE THE COURT IS DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR AN INDEPENDENT MEDICAL EXAMINATION OF PLAINTIFF AND THERE APPEARING GOOD CAUSE EXISTING THEREFOR SAID MOTION IS GRANTED AND THE INDEPENDENT MEDICAL EXAMINATION OF THE PLAINTIFF SHALL GO FORWARD BY JUNE 30, 2018. THE DEFENDANT SHALL PREPARE THE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 AND PRESENT IT TO JUDGE PRO TEM DIANNE PEEBLES FOR THE COURT'S REVIEW AND SIGNATURE. JUDGE PRO TEM: DIANNE PEEBLES, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, MATTER NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion For Order To Compel, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-25-2018 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Plaintiff James Quedado's Motion For Order To Compel, And For Sanctions is off calendar as duplicate entry. =(302/JPT/RBU)

Notice Of Motion For Order To Compel Defendants Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-25-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Vadim Nebuchin, Esq. of Callaway & Wolf, Tel. 415.541.0300, appearing for the plaintiff/moving party James Quedado;
Edward J. Rodzewich, Esq. of Hartsuyker Stratman & Williams-Abrego, appearing for the defendant/opposing party Elizabeth Sinclair.

Both sides stipulate that the matter may be heard by Judge Pro Tem Dianne Peebles.

Matter called, argument held.

Ruling: At the conclusion of argument, the tentative ruling on is adopted as follows: Plaintiff Quedado's Motion to Compel Defendant's Further Responses to Form Interrogatories and Defendant's Opposition thereto having been duly considered the motion to compel is hereby GRANTED and the defendant shall serve verified responses to Interrogatory numbers 16.3, 16.4, 16.5 and 16.6 by July 30, 2018. The request for sanction is hereby DENIED (CCP 2023.040, Notice failed to state against whom the sanctions are sought). Plaintiff shall prepare the ORDER in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and submit it to Judge Pro Tem Dianne Peebles for the Court's review and signature.

Judge Pro Tem: Dianne Peebles, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter Not Reported. =(302/JPT/HEK)

Notice Of Motion To Reopen Discovery
Off Calendar Apr-25-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Edward J. Rodzewich, Esq. of Hartsuyker Stratman & Williams-Abrego, appearing for the defendant/moving party Elizabeth Sinclair;
Vadim Nebuchin, Esq. of Callaway & Wolf, Tel. 415.541.0300, appearing for the plaintiff/opposing party James Quedado.

Both sides stipulate that the matter may be heard by Judge Pro Tem Dianne Peebles.

Matter called, argument held.

At the conclusion of argument, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows: Defendant Sinclair's Motion to Reopen Discovery and Plaintiff Quedado's Opposition thereto were duly considered. It is noted that the parties have stipulated to Defendant's request for production of all relevant medical records and bills generated in 2018. Before the court is Defendant's request for an Independent Medical Examination of Plaintiff and there appearing good cause existing therefor said Motion is GRANTED and the Independent Medical Examination of the Plaintiff shall go forward by June 30, 2018. The Defendant shall prepare the Order in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and present it to Judge Pro Tem Dianne Peebles for the Court's review and signature.

Judge Pro Tem: Dianne Peebles, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter Not Reported. =(302/JPT/HEK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-06-2017 Assigned To Arbitration On Sep-18-17

Notice Of Motion To Quash Subpoenas; And For A Protective Order; And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-30-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Ruling: PLAINTIFF JAMES QUEDADO Motion To Quash Subpoenas; And For A Protective Order; And For Monetary Sanctions is granted in part. Plaintiff has represented that the only injured body parts for which he seeks damages are injuries related to his upper and lower extremities. Therefore, absent further order of the court, plaintiff can't seek damages for injury to any other body parts. Only records related to plaintiff's upper and lower extremities, including but not limited to the mobility of the upper and lower extremities, shall be produced to defendant. The service defendant has used, Ronsin, shall Bates stamp all of the records produced by each subpoenaed Kaiser and produce them only to plaintiff's counsel's office to allow him to perform a first look of the records. Within two weeks of receipt of the records from Ronsin, plaintiff's counsel shall serve the subpoenaed records on defense counsel with a declaration. The declaration shall be made under penalty of perjury and state the page numbers of the pages plaintiff's counsel has removed or redacted form the subpoenaed records, and the bases for such removal or redaction. If defense counsel disagrees with plaintiff's counsel's removal or redaction of the records, defense counsel shall contact Bruce Highman, judge pro tem, to arrange a telephonic hearing between the judge pro tem and the parties. Each side's request for sanctions is denied. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: Bruce Highman; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.
